title: cannabis sativa subsp. indica
intro: Cannabis sativa subsp. indica, a subspecies of the cannabis plant, is a variety of hemp that is native to the Indian subcontinent and is known for its high levels of cannabidiol (CBD) and low levels of tetrahydrocannabinol (THC). The medicinal properties of Cannabis sativa subsp. indica have been used to treat a range of health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and anxiety, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anxiolytic effects. The health benefits of this plant include reduced chronic pain, improved sleep quality, and alleviation of symptoms associated with multiple sclerosis and other neurological disorders. Cannabis sativa subsp. indica is often used in herbal preparations such as CBD oil, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be administered orally or applied directly to the skin for therapeutic relief.
